government_schemes.name: PM-KISAN (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi)
government_schemes.benefit: ₹6,000 per year direct income support in 3 equal installments of ₹2,000
government_schemes.eligibility: Small and marginal farmers with cultivable land up to 2 hectares
government_schemes.how_to_apply: Visit Common Service Centre (CSC) or register at pmkisan.gov.in with Aadhaar, land records, and bank account

government_schemes.name: PMFBY (Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana)
government_schemes.benefit: Crop insurance against natural calamities, pests, and diseases. Premium: 2% for Kharif, 1.5% for Rabi, 5% for horticulture
government_schemes.eligibility: All farmers growing notified crops in notified areas
government_schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through bank, CSC, or on pmfby.gov.in before cutoff date
government_schemes.important: Loanee farmers are automatically enrolled. Non-loanee must apply separately.

government_schemes.name: PMKSY (Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana)
government_schemes.benefit: Subsidy on drip and sprinkler irrigation installation. 55–80% subsidy for small/marginal farmers.
government_schemes.tagline: Har Khet Ko Pani, More Crop Per Drop
government_schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through Agriculture Department or at pmksy.gov.in

government_schemes.name: Soil Health Card Scheme
government_schemes.benefit: Free soil testing and health card with nutrient status and fertilizer recommendations for each farm
government_schemes.how_to_apply: Submit soil sample at KVK, Agriculture Department or mobile lab. Collect card in 2–3 weeks.

government_schemes.name: eNAM (National Agriculture Market)
government_schemes.benefit: Online trading platform connecting farmers to buyers across India. Better price discovery, reduced intermediary cost.
government_schemes.registration: Register at enam.gov.in with Aadhaar and bank details

government_schemes.name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
government_schemes.benefit: Short-term credit for crop production at 4% interest rate (with interest subvention). Cover for input costs.
government_schemes.limit: Up to ₹3 lakh at 4% (with ₹1.5 lakh interest subvention)
government_schemes.eligibility: All farmers, sharecroppers, tenant farmers, SHG members
government_schemes.how_to_apply: Apply at nearest nationalized/cooperative/RRB bank with land records and Aadhaar

government_schemes.name: PKVY (Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana)
government_schemes.benefit: ₹50,000/ha over 3 years for conversion to organic farming. Covers organic input costs, certification, and marketing.
government_schemes.focus: Soil health improvement, organic certification (PGS-India), market linkage

government_schemes.name: RKVY (Rashtriya Krishi Vikas Yojana)
government_schemes.benefit: State-flexible scheme for agriculture development – farm equipment, horticulture, animal husbandry, fisheries

selling_channels.description: State Agricultural Produce Market Committee mandis provide transparent auction. Good for large volumes.
selling_channels.tip: Know the current mandi prices before selling. Use mandi mobile apps or agmarknet.gov.in.

selling_channels.description: Online mandi – sell from your local mandi but reach buyers across India.
selling_channels.tip: Clean, sorted, graded produce gets better price on eNAM.

selling_channels.description: Collective selling through FPO eliminates intermediaries. FPOs can also negotiate processing and export contracts.
selling_channels.tip: Join or form an FPO for collective bargaining power.

selling_channels.description: Pre-agreed price with companies (food processing, export houses). Reduces market risk.
Sell directly at weekly markets, own outlet, or through apps (BigBasket, Dehaat, Ninjacart).
selling_channels.tip: Add value (cleaning, grading, packing) to get premium price.

price_information_sources: NCDEX live prices for commodity futures
